headlines.title: Body found inside burned home near scene of deadly shooting in Santa Clara
headlines.description: A body was found inside the burned-down house near the scene of Friday’s deadly shooting in Santa Clara, the police department announced on Saturday. On Friday, a person reported hearing about eight or nine gunshots at about 12:30 p.m. around Serra and Enright Avenues, according to initial police communications. Officers were heard saying they believed a man was walking to his car, possibly from a house, when another man shot him multiple times. Shortly after, the house went up in flames. Police confirmed the victim died at the scene. That evening, police said officers, with the assistance of drones, found a body inside the back of the burned home. At the time, authorities said the home had significant structural damage and still presented ongoing hazards. On Saturday at around 11 a.m., the home was determined safe for entry from the Santa Clara Fire Department, officials said. Authorities said investigators were then able to approach the body. The police department noted it is believed this was a targeted attack with no further threat to public safety. The circumstances associated with this incident are under investigation.

headlines.title: Source: Steve Kerr agrees to two-year contract to return as Warriors coach
headlines.description: Steve Kerr isn’t going anywhere. Despite early speculation that the 2025-26 NBA season was Kerr’s last with the Warriors, the 60-year-old coach will return for at least two more seasons. Kerr has reached an agreement in principle with Golden State on a new two-year contract, a source confirmed to NBC Sports Bay Area’s Dalton Johnson on Saturday. ESPN’s Shams Charania was the first to report the news of the agreement between Kerr and the Warriors. The new deal will keep Steve Kerr as the highest-paid coach in the NBA annually, Charania also reported, citing sources. Last week, NBC Sports Bay Area’s Monte Poole reported that people close to the Warriors who initially believed Kerr would leave had changed course and indicated a “reasonable possibility” he returns. That reporting came a few days after ESPN’s Charania, Anthony Slater and Ramona Shelburne reported that Kerr met with Warriors CEO Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy on Monday, April 24. Per ESPN, those initial discussions were deemed “productive” by both sides. And those productive talks turned into a new contract for Kerr. But, for a few weeks at the end of the season, it was a toss-up as to whether the long-time coach and the only team he has ever coached would get to this point. The Warriors’ 2025-26 NBA season ended with a disappointing play-in loss to the Phoenix Suns on April 17, and after the game, Kerr admitted he wasn’t sure about what the future held. “My plan is to take a little time, I don’t know,” Kerr told reporters. “Take a week or two and eventually sit down and talk with [owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy]. We’ve always had a great partnership and collaboration and just see where they are and I’ll tell them where I am, and we’ll talk about what’s next for the Warriors, what the plan is this offseason. And we will come to a collaborative decision on what’s next. “I don’t know what’s going to happen. I still love coaching, but I get it. These jobs all have an expiration date. There’s a run that happens. When the run ends, it’s sometimes it’s time for new blood and new ideas and all that. If that’s the case, then I will be just nothing but grateful for the most amazing opportunity any person could have to coach this franchise, in front of our fans in the Bay and to coach Steph Curry and coach [Draymond Green] and the whole group. So, it may still go on. It may not. I don’t know at this point. But we all need to step away a little bit and reconvene.” Towards the end of the Warriors’ season-ending loss, Kerr embraced franchise icons Steph Curry and Draymond Green, and left them with a simple but ominous message. “I don’t know what’s going to happen next, but I love you guys to death,” Kerr told Curry and Green. “Thank you.” Less than a week after that moment between Kerr, Curry and Green, multiple sources told NBC Sports Bay Area’s Monte Poole that, barring a renewed faith that prompted a change of heart, the belief was that the 60-year-old coach wouldn’t return for a 13th season. But after talking with Lacob and Dunleavy, Kerr has decided to stick around for at least one more season. Kerr initially was hired in May 2014, and in 12 seasons with the Warriors, won four championships (2015, ’17, ’18 and ’22) with a dynastic core of Curry, Green and Klay Thompson. Throughout Golden State’s disappointing 2025-26 season, Kerr remained steadfast in his belief in the potential of the team’s roster when healthy. The Warriors were hampered by injuries, specifically to Curry and star forward Jimmy Butler, but now with an entire offseason ahead to rest up and re-tool the roster, Golden State — led again by Kerr — will have another opportunity to compete for a fifth championship next season. Download and follow the Dubs Talk Podcast This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Police investigate shooting involving 2 vehicles in East Palo Alto
headlines.description: Police are investigating a shooting involving two vehicles that left two people hurt in East Palo Alto on Saturday. Authorities said officers responded to a ShotSpotter activation reporting gunfire in the 2300 block of Clarke Avenue at around 3:40 p.m. Officers later received additional reports of a shooting involving two vehicles in the area. During the initial response to the incident, police said they also received a separate call regarding a victim suffering from a gunshot wound at a residential address in East Palo Alto. Officers went to the area and confirmed the victim was connected to the shooting. Police confirmed the shooting had occurred, and said both victims were listed in stable condition. They added the circumstances surrounding the shooting are under investigation.

headlines.title: Mexican families reunite in San Jose after being separated for years
headlines.description: There was a joyful reunion in the south bay on Saturday between Mexican parents and their children, who’ve been separated for years. The reunion was all part of a program by the Federación Jalisco Internacional. The social initiative group helps and relatives who live in Mexico to travel to the U.S. under temporary tourist visas. These families have been separated for at least a decade when their children migrated to the U.S. They were reunited once again in San Jose. “He hasn’t seen his family members for the last 30 years, so to see how resilient he’s been, to finally see his own family has been a privilege and honor to see,” said Jocelyn Negrete Arroyo. As part of the reunification process, the federation provides the families with social and emotional support to reduce the impact of separation, caused by migration.

headlines.title: Police activity shuts down eastbound I-580 in Livermore
headlines.description: Police activity had shut down both directions of Interstate 580, east of Isabel Avenue, in Livermore, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP Dublin on Friday afternoon reported just before 3 p.m. both directions of the freeway were still shut down. Just before 3:30 p.m., CHP announced only westbound lanes were opened. Just before 4 p.m. CHP said all lanes were reopened. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Deadly shooting near scene of Santa Clara house fire
headlines.description: Santa Clara police on Friday were searching a neighborhood for a suspect who shot and killed a man before possibly setting a house on fire. According to initial police communications, someone reported hearing about eight or nine gunshots at about 12:30 p.m. in the area of Serra and Enright avenues. Officers were heard saying they believed a man was walking to his car, possibly from a house, when another man shot him multiple times. Shortly thereafter, the house went up in flames. Firefighters used ladder trucks to douse the burning structure from above. Police confirmed the victim later died at the scene. Neighbors who said they knew the victim were horrified. “It’s horrible, it’s horrible,” Virginia Rogers said. “My neighbor’s husband got killed. They’re a very close family. It’s very, very sad. For her to find out, come home, and find your husband dead on the lawn, it’s horrible.” A resident who owns the house directly behind the burning home called the scene hectic. “I could see the smoke over my roof line,” the resident said. “The police had pulled up and had weapons drawn and ordered my wife and my daughter to evacuate the house, which they did pretty, pretty quickly.” The resident said his wife heard multiple gunshots. As of late Friday afternoon, police were searching homes in the area for the shooter. They plan to enter the house that caught fire to conduct a search there. As of Saturday afternoon, police were still on the scene investigating, and a portion of Enright Avenue remained blocked off. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Woman dies after solo-vehicle crash in Antioch
headlines.description: A woman died following a single-vehicle crash in Antioch early Saturday morning, according to the police department. Antioch police said officers responded to a report of a single-vehicle collision in the area of G and Drake streets at around 1 a.m. There, officers found a 51-year-old woman who had been ejected from a vehicle, according to authorities. They said the woman was taken to the hospital with major injuries and she later died. The police department said preliminary information from witnesses and evidence suggests speed and impairment may have been contributing factors. They add, however, that this is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: San Francisco man convicted in fraudulent auto insurance scheme
headlines.description: A federal jury has convicted a San Francisco man of conspiracy, wire fraud, and money laundering in connection with a scheme involving fraudulent insurance claims on already-wrecked vehicles, prosecutors said. Colin Jackson, 39, was found guilty Wednesday following a seven-day trial before U.S. District Judge Trina L. Thompson, according to the U.S. Attorney’s Office. Prosecutors said Jackson conspired with others, including previously convicted defendant Kirill Afanasyev, to defraud an automobile insurance company by filing false claims involving damaged cars that had already been wrecked. According to prosecutors, evidence presented at trial showed Jackson obtained an insurance policy in 2018 on a vehicle that was undrivable and made false statements on the application, including the car’s estimated annual mileage. Months later, Jackson and Afanasyev submitted a fraudulent claim involving a staged accident, prosecutors said. The insurance company, unaware the vehicle had already been wrecked, approved the claim and paid Jackson about $27,000, prosecutors said. Authorities said the pair carried out a similar scheme in 2017 involving another damaged vehicle, resulting in an insurance payout of about $30,000. Jackson is scheduled to be sentenced Sept. 25. Federal officials said more than a dozen defendants have pleaded guilty or been convicted as part of an ongoing investigation into automobile insurance fraud and a related arson conspiracy.

headlines.title: Golden State Valkyries kick off regular season with a win and high hopes
headlines.description: The Golden State Valkyries played the first regular-season game of their second season on Friday night. The team played in Seattle, where they were able to lock in a win over the Seattle Storm. The Valkyries hosted a watch party for Bay Area fans at the Thrive City Plaza outside Chase Center in San Francisco. Several hundred people showed up to watch the game on the big screen, including many season-ticket holders. As the team starts its second season, fans say their historic first season built up a loyal fanbase. “I see a lot more purple around, and it’s always really exciting to see someone else wearing Valkyries [gear] here cause I just kind of point at them — and that camaraderie of a whole new fanbase to geek out with,” said season ticketholder Mace Mamlock of San Francisco. The Valkyries sold out game after game and became the first WNBA expansion team to make it to the playoffs in their first season. A recent CNBC ranking of team valuations showed that the team is now worth one billion dollars, which is a WNBA record. The team’s impact is also being felt by businesses around Chase Center. Parisha Elviano with Fikscue Barbecue at Thrive City said of the Valkyries that businesses feel “really blessed to have them beginning their second season here, and a lot of crowds coming in.” The team is also leaning into Bay Area basketball history. On Friday, they unveiled their new ‘Rebel’ jerseys which pay homage to past women’s teams like the San Francisco Pioneers and the San Jose Lasers. The Valkyries will play their home opener game on Sunday evening, and businesses nearby said they expect the game to draw big crowds to Chase Center.

headlines.title: Candidates for California governor talk about housing crisis at forum in Oakland
headlines.description: Five candidates running to become California’s next governor were in Oakland on Friday to talk about one of the biggest issues facing Bay Area voters: Housing. The forum was hosted at the Henry J. Kaiser Center and focused on the state’s housing crisis. NBC Bay Area was able to speak to four of the candidates about why this issue is central to their campaigns. Those candidates were Matt Mahan, Xavier Becerra, Katie Porter and Tom Steyer. The fifth candidate decided not to talk. The two leading Republican candidates for governor were also invited but decided not to attend. The discussion comes less than a month before the June primary, with mail-in voting already underway and many Bay Area voters feeling priced out. All four candidates in attendance agreed California has a housing affordability crisis. Pete Suratos has the full report in the video player above.

headlines.title: Contra Costa County anti-sprawl measure sparks debate
headlines.description: People in Contra Costa County will be voting on a measure that’s pitting conservationists against housing advocates. The county has had a voter-approved boundary protecting open space for decades. Voters will decide whether to extend it for another 25 years. Real Estate Attorney and San Ramon resident Vince Moita says the county is dealing with a housing affordability crisis. He says an urban sprawl measure on the ballot, Measure A, will only make it worse. “When you look at Contra Costa County 65% of the land will be restricted for at least the next 25 years to be non-urban,” Moita said. Measure A extends a decades old policy that would protect 65% of Contra Costa County’s land from development until at least 2051, leaving in place an urban limit line. “If we didn’t have this line, it would make it a lot easier for developers to build in areas that are the worst suited for development,” said Juan Pablo Galvan Martinez with Save Mt Diablo. Galvan Martinez says it’s critical to protect the county’s open space that’s home to countless wildlife species. “You have an incredible number of species of wildlife plant and other sorts of organisms that not only live here. Many of which are found nowhere else on earth,” he said. Galvan Martinez says the urban limit line has worked well for the last 36 years at preventing urban sprawl that creates traffic and keeping developers from building in high fire zones. “Most of the county outside the urban limit line is very high or high fire hazard zones meaning they’re the most likely to burn and the least suitable for development,” he said. Moita says it’s time to do away with the urban limit line and make way for growth. It’ll be up to voters to decide on June 2.

headlines.title: 1994 U.S. Men's National Soccer Team honored at America SCORES gala in San Jose
headlines.description: The soccer community came together Friday to honor the iconic 1994 U.S. Men’s National Soccer Team during the America SCORES Soccer Legacy Gala in downtown San Jose. The event, presented by Coca-Cola and Airbnb, started at 5 p.m. at the Signia by Hilton. The event honored the “iconic legacy” of the 1994 U.S. Men’s National Team, whose “groundbreaking performance ignited modern American soccer and helped shape its future,” according to organizers. Organizers said America SCORES was born thanks to that legacy, and began transforming young lives through soccer, poetry and service. Organizers added the gathering marks the 25th anniversary of the Bay Area out-of-school program.
